# SeenBy — Case Study Template

> Use this once a client shows measurable score improvement.  
> Two versions: internal (full detail) and external (anonymised, for proposals + LinkedIn).  
> Fill the internal version first. Derive the external version from it.

---

## EXTERNAL VERSION (Use in Proposals, LinkedIn, Website)

> Anonymise unless client consented to being named.  
> Focus on the insight and the result — not the process details.

---

### One-Liner (for proposal "Case Studies" section)

> **[Industry] brand, [city]:** Went from a visibility score of [X] to [X] in [X] months — moved from invisible in ChatGPT to being recommended for [X] of [Y] target queries.

---

### Short Version (150 words — for proposals)

**Client:** [e.g., "A mid-sized professional services firm in Kuala Lumpur"] (anonymised)

**Challenge:** When potential customers asked AI for the best [their service] in [their city], [Client] was not mentioned — their main competitor appeared in 3 out of 4 platforms. Their AI Visibility Score was [X]/100.

**What we did:** Ran a full baseline scan to identify the gap, delivered the AI Readiness Toolkit (llms.txt, schema markup, updated robots.txt), and flagged one AI hallucination about their location. Over the following 90 days, the client implemented the toolkit and added a competitor comparison page to their site.

**Result:** Score improved from [X] to [X]/100 — a [X]-point increase in [X] months. The client went from invisible in ChatGPT to recommended for [X] of their [Y] core queries. The competitor gap closed from [X] points to [X] points.

**Key insight:** [One sentence — e.g., "Implementing the three toolkit files alone accounted for roughly half the score improvement — the most direct technical lever available."]

---

### LinkedIn Post Version

*(Adapt Post 5 from `linkedin-posts.md` with real numbers once available)*

---

## When to Publish a Case Study

Publish when:
- [ ] Score improved by at least 10 points
- [ ] At least 2 months of data
- [ ] At least one concrete metric beyond just the score (query wins, competitor gap, hallucination fixed)
- [ ] Client has approved (or anonymisation is thorough enough)

Do not publish if:
- Results are inconclusive or driven by external factors (platform algorithm change, competitor went offline)
- Client explicitly asked not to be referenced
